notes.note: Theopompus of Cnidus, a learned Greek who was a favourite and agent of Caesar; his news here reports Caesar's intention to stay in Rome rather than leave for the East.

notes.anchor: his own laws would be disregarded
notes.note: Caesar feared that the body of legislation he had pushed through as dictator would be ignored if he left Rome, citing the way his sumptuary law was already being flouted.

notes.anchor: just as the sumptuary law had been
notes.note: Caesar's Lex Iulia sumptuaria, a statute capping private expenditure on luxury and dining; its open neglect is Caesar's example of how his laws lapse in his absence.

P. Cornelius Lentulus Spinther the younger; his divorce from his wife Metella is reported as the latest piece of Roman gossip. Metella herself cannot be matched to a registered entity and is left untagged.
